revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:合祀]]


{{Source citation|date|March 2011|qq=}}
'''Gōshi''' (合祀) is a term used in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}.
==Overview==
It refers to the practice of enshrining the {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|de|verehrte Gottheit|ja|祭神|zh|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}}(s) of one {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} together at another shrine (a practice known as "寄宮"). Alternatively, it denotes the condition in which multiple enshrined deities are worshiped within a single shrine (known as an aiden 相殿).
It is also referred to as '''Gōsai''' (合祭, ごうさい、がふさい).
In the case of the former type of amalgamation, there are three forms:
* The main hall amalgamation, in which the deities are enshrined together in the [[Honden]] of the shrine
* The inner grounds amalgamation, in which the original shrine is relocated within the shrine grounds as a [https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Keidai-sha Keidai-sha] after being moved to the {{ill|shrine grounds|de|Schreinareal|ja|境内|zh|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine grounds|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11429002}}.
* The off-site grounds amalgamation, in which the original shrine is relocated to a detached section of the shrine grounds as a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=Setsumatsusha|1=Setsumatsusha}}.
From the Meiji ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}) to the Taishō ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}}) period, under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|qid=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Consolidation Policy|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, many shrines were abolished as a result of amalgamation. Among these abolished shrines, some later had their enshrined deities restored (a process known as re-enshrinement) and were rebuilt.
==Related Topics==
* {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|qid=Q1107129|lt=Sōja shrine|1=Sōja shrine}}
* {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}
* {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|qid=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Consolidation Policy|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}
==External Links==
* {{CRD|1000302942|en|I am searching for a book that describes the purpose of the shrine amalgamations carried out in the 40s of the Meiji era.|de|Ich suche ein Buch, das den Zweck der in den 40er Jahren der Meiji-Periode durchgeführten Schreinamalgamierungen beschreibt.|ja|明治40年代におこなわれた神社の合祀について、その目的を書いた本を探している。|zh|我正在寻找一本描述明治40年代进行的神社合祀目的的书。|ill|Chiba Prefectural Central Library|de|Zentralbibliothek der Präfektur Chiba|ja|千葉県立中央図書館|zh|千葉県立中央図書館|qq=}}
{{Shinto navigation|qq=}}
{{Shinto stub|qq=}}
